mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that absorbs higher financing costs, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive 20-day returns in >70% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms broad-based labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward windows in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day returns, with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain disruptions, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops below -0.5% MoM are followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of cases.
